entry.title: Collections - PagerDuty
entry.content:
Coming Soon​

PagerDuty is a leading digital operations management platform that empowers teams to proactively manage and respond to incidents in real time. By providing actionable insights, automation, and collaboration tools, PagerDuty ensures business continuity and exceptional customer experiences. It centralizes alerts, streamlines incident resolution, and enables teams to work seamlessly across the entire organization, minimizing downtime and optimizing operational efficiency.

Identities ❌ | Accounts ✔️ | Groups ✔️ | App Roles ✔️ | Entitlements ❌

Teams are a way to organize users and objects in PagerDuty. They can be used to control who has access to different objects, such as escalation policies, users, schedules, and services. Teams can also be used to customize the PagerDuty web app by filtering for information relevant to the team's associated objects.

User roles define the level of access that a user has to PagerDuty objects.

RoleDescription
adminHas full access to all objects and configurations in the account. Can create, update, and delete users, teams, schedules, escalation policies, services, and integrations. Can also manage user permissions.
limited_userCan view objects and configurations, but cannot make any modifications.
observerCan view objects, but cannot make any modifications.
ownerHas full access to objects and configurations within their team. Can create, update, and delete users, schedules, escalation policies, and services. Can also manage user permissions within their team.
read_only_userCan view objects, but cannot make any modifications.
restricted_accessCan only view incidents and alerts that they are directly involved in.
read_only_limited_userCan only view objects that they have been granted access to.
userCan view objects and configurations, but cannot make any modifications.

entry.title: Collections - Tableau
entry.summary: Tableau is a powerful data visualization tool that transforms complex datasets into interactive and insightful visuals, aiding informed decision-making. Its user-friendly interface enables easy creation of charts, graphs, and dashboards, enhancing data exploration and presentation across various industries and professions.Identities ❌ | Accounts ✔️ | Groups ✔️ | App Roles ✔️ | Entitlements ❌
entry.content:
Tableau is a powerful data visualization tool that transforms complex datasets into interactive and insightful visuals, aiding informed decision-making. Its user-friendly interface enables easy creation of charts, graphs, and dashboards, enhancing data exploration and presentation across various industries and professions.

Identities ❌ | Accounts ✔️ | Groups ✔️ | App Roles ✔️ | Entitlements ❌

In Tableau Cloud, users, groups, and site roles are used to control access to content and features.

Users are individual people who have access to a Tableau Cloud site. Users can be assigned different site roles, which determine what they can do on the site.

Groups are collections of users. Groups can be used to simplify the process of assigning site roles to multiple users.

Site roles define the level of access a user has to a Tableau Cloud site. Site roles include:

  • Viewer: Viewers can view published content, but they cannot create or modify content.
  • Explorer: Explorers can create and modify content, but they cannot publish content to the site.
  • Creator: Creators can create, modify, and publish content to the site.
  • Site Administrator Creator: Site Administrator Creators have all the capabilities of Creators, plus the ability to manage the site's users and settings.
  • Site Administrator Explorer: Site Administrator Explorers have all the capabilities of Explorers, plus the ability to manage the site's users and settings.

Table of Contents​

  • Application
  • Account Collector
  • Entitlement Collector : Role Members
  • Application Setup
  • Token Authentication
  • Custom Web Service : Site Roles

Application​

Steps

  1. Login to RSA G&L console as System Administrator.
  2. Navigate to Resources > Application.
  3. Click on Create Application and select Other Application.
  4. Enter the details as shown below.
  5. Click Finish.

Account Collector​

This section explains the process for configuring an account collector for the Tableau application. We will use the RSA G&L Generic REST collector API for this purpose.

Steps

  1. Login to RSA G&L console as System Administrator.
  2. Navigate to Resources > Applications.
  3. Click on Tableau.
  4. Navigate to Collectors tab and click on Create Account Collector. Enter the details as shown below.

  5. Click Next and enter the connection details.


    The URL contains the site URL https://prod-useast-b.online.tableau.com (for my instance) which needs to be updated to suit your deployment and the site it. Site ID can be retrieved from the response to the token authentication request.

  6. Click Next. Select the data types that the collector will be collecting from Tableau

  7. Click Next. On the Account Data page, enter the following configurations.




    Request
    MethodGET
    URL/users
    Query Parameters
    fieldsid,email,lastLogin
    Request Headers
    Acceptapplication/json
    AuthorizationBearer ${AuthenticationToken}
    Response Mappings
    AccountId$.users.*.*.email
    Id$.users.*.*.id
    LastLogin$.users.*.*.lastLogin

  8. Click Next. On the Group Data page, enter the following configurations.




    Request
    MethodGET
    URL/groups
    Query Parameters
    fieldsid,name
    Request Headers
    Acceptapplication/json
    AuthorizationBearer ${AuthenticationToken}
    Response Mappings
    GroupId$.groups.*.*.name
    Id$.groups.*.*.id
  9. Click Next. On the Account Group Membership page, enter the following configurations.




    Request
    MethodGET
    URL/groups/${Id}/users
    Request Headers
    Acceptapplication/json
    AuthorizationBearer ${AuthenticationToken}
    Response Mappings
    Account Identifier$.users.user.*.id
  10. Click Next. In the Map Collector Attributes to Account Attributes, map the collected account attributes.

  11. Click Next. In the Map Collector Attributes to Account Mapping Attributes, map the user reference attribute.

  12. Click Next. In the Map Collector Attributes to Group Attributes, map the collected group attributes.

  13. Click Next. In the User Resolution Rules, map the user to account.

  14. Click Next. In the Member Account Resolution Rules, map the account.

💡 Info: This value MUST always be mapped to the Account Name for any generic REST collector.

  1. Click Finish. Use the Test function to make sure the configurations are accurate.

Entitlement Collector : Role Members​

This section explains the process for configuring an entitlement collector to collect site roles and assignments from the Tableau application. We will use the RSA G&L Generic REST collector for this purpose.

info

Since Tableau does not provide an API for listing site roles, we will use a custom web service for the EDC. Refer to Custom Web Service : Site Roles for more information.

Steps

  1. Login to RSA G&L console as AveksaAdmin.
  2. Navigate to Resources > Applications.
  3. Click on Tableau.
  4. Navigate to Collectors tab and click on Create Entitlement Collector. Enter the details as shown below.

  5. Click Next and enter the connection details as shown in Step 5 of Account Collector. 💡 Note that the Use Base URL for all API(s) will be unchecked.
  6. Click Next. Select the data types that the collector will be collecting from Tableau.

  7. Click Next. On the App Role Data page, enter the following configurations.




    Request
    MethodGET
    URLhttps://instance.securid.com/aveksa/custom/external_jsp/TableauSiteRolesv2.jsp
    Request Headers
    Acceptapplication/json
    Response Mappings
    ApplicationRoleId$.roles.*.role
    Description$.roles.*.description
  8. Click Next. On the App-Role Account Membership collection page, enter the following configurations.




    Request
    MethodGET
    URLhttps://instance.online.tableau.com/api/3.2/sites/SITE_ID/users
    Query Parameters
    fieldsid,siteRole
    Request Headers
    Acceptapplication/json
    AuthorizationBearer ${AuthenticationToken}
    Response Mappings
    Application Role Id$.users.user.*.siteRole
    Account Identifier$.users.user.*.id
  9. Click Next. In the Map Collector Attributes to App Role Attributes, map the collected account attributes.
  10. Click Next. In the Account Evaluation, map the account collector to account value.

  11. Click Next and then click Finish. Use the Test function to make sure the configurations are accurate

Application Setup​

This section explains the process of setting up Personal Access Token (PAT) in Tableau for use within the collections process.

Steps

  1. Login to Tableau site https://instance.online.tableau.com/#/site/SITE/home.
  2. By default, Personal Access Token (PAT) is disabled.
  3. Navigate to Settings in the navigation panel and click on General tab.
  4. Go to Personal Access Tokens section and enable PAT.

  5. Navigate to Users in the navigation panel and click on the user whose PAT needs to be generated.
  6. Click on Settings tab.
  7. Go to Personal Access Tokens section. Provide a suitable token name and click Create Token.

  8. Copy the Secret and click Done.

Token Authentication​

This section explains the process of setting up RSA G&L ADC with token authentication.

Steps

  1. Login to RSA G&L console as System Administrator.
  2. Create or Edit a generic REST ADC that will be using Token authentication.
  3. On the Connection Details page, configure the following.



    Request
    MethodPOST
    Token URL https://YOUR_INSTANCE/api/3.13/auth/signin
    UsernameYOUR_PAT_NAME
    PasswordYOUR_PAT
    Request Headers
    Acceptapplication/json
    Content-Typeapplication/xml
    Request Body
    <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
    <tsRequest>
    <credentials personalAccessTokenName="${TokenUsername}" personalAccessTokenSecret="${TokenPassword}">
    <site contentUrl="rsaps" />
    </credentials>
    </tsRequest>
    Response Mappings
    Response Path$.credentials.token
  4. Save the collector configuration & test.

Custom Web Service : Site Roles​

Since Tableau does not provide an API for listing site roles, we will use a custom JSP that will publish the site roles.

Steps

  1. Open a text editor and save the contents below as TableauSiteRolesv2.jsp.
<%@ page language="java" contentType="application/json" %>
<%
    String jsonData = "{"
        + "\"roles\": ["
        + "{\"role\":\"Creator\",\"description\":\"Users with this role can create and publish content such as workbooks and data sources on the Tableau site.\"},"
        + "{\"role\":\"Explorer\",\"description\":\"Users assigned as Explorers can interact with and explore content on the Tableau site. They can view and interact with dashboards and reports but do not have the ability to create or publish new content.\"},"
        + "{\"role\":\"ExplorerCanPublish\",\"description\":\"Similar to the \\\"Explorer\\\" role, users with this role can explore content on the site. Additionally, they have the ability to create and publish their own content.\"},"
        + "{\"role\":\"ServerAdministrator\",\"description\":\"Users with this role have full administrative control over the entire Tableau Server. They can manage users, groups, projects, data sources, and server-wide settings.\"},"
        + "{\"role\":\"SiteAdministratorExplorer\",\"description\":\"Site Administrators have full control over a specific site. For the Explorer role, this means they can manage users, content, and site-specific settings.\"},"
        + "{\"role\":\"SiteAdministratorCreator\",\"description\":\"Similar to SiteAdministratorExplorer, Site Administrators with the Creator role can manage users, content, and site settings. They also have the ability to create new content.\"},"
        + "{\"role\":\"Unlicensed\",\"description\":\"Users assigned this role do not have access to content on the Tableau site. It is typically used for users who do not need access to Tableau resources.\"},"
        + "{\"role\":\"ReadOnly\",\"description\":\"Users with this role can view content on the Tableau site but do not have the ability to interact with or modify it. They can consume dashboards and reports without making changes.\"},"
        + "{\"role\":\"Viewer\",\"description\":\"Similar to the ReadOnly role, Viewers can view and interact with content on the Tableau site, but they also have the ability to make changes to views and save personal copies of them.\"}"
        + "]"
        + "}";
%>

<%
    response.setContentType("application/json");
    out.print(jsonData);
%>
  1. Login to RSA G&L console as AveksaAdmin.
  2. Navigate to Admin > User Interface.
  3. Click on Files.
  4. Select External JSP Pages and click on Upload.
  5. Select the TableauSiteRolesv2.jsp and click OK.
  6. Verify by accessing this URL in the browser https://instance.securid.com/aveksa/custom/external_jsp/TableauSiteRolesv2.jsp
  1. Tableau APIs

    ↩

entry.title: Collections - Dynatrace
entry.summary: Dynatrace is a software intelligence company that specializes in application performance management (APM) and observability solutions for modern cloud environments. Its platform provides real-time insights into the performance of applications and the underlying infrastructure that supports them. Dynatrace's primary goal is to help organizations ensure the optimal functioning of their digital services and applications, especially in complex and dynamic environments.Identities ❌ | Accounts ✔️ | Groups ✔️ | App Roles ❌ | Entitlements ❌
entry.content:
Dynatrace is a software intelligence company that specializes in application performance management (APM) and observability solutions for modern cloud environments. Its platform provides real-time insights into the performance of applications and the underlying infrastructure that supports them. Dynatrace's primary goal is to help organizations ensure the optimal functioning of their digital services and applications, especially in complex and dynamic environments.

Identities ❌ | Accounts ✔️ | Groups ✔️ | App Roles ❌ | Entitlements ❌

Table of Contents​

  • Application
  • Account Collector
  • Application Setup
  • Token Authentication

Application​

Steps

  1. Login to RSA G&L console as System Administrator.
  2. Navigate to Resources > Application.
  3. Click on Create Application and select Other Application.
  4. Enter the details as shown below.
  5. Click Finish.

Account Collector​

This section explains the process for configuring an account collector for the Dynatrace application. We will use the RSA G&L Generic REST collector API for this purpose.

Steps

  1. Login to RSA G&L console as System Administrator.
  2. Navigate to Resources > Applications.
  3. Click on Dynatrace.
  4. Navigate to Collectors tab and click on Create Account Collector. Enter the details as shown below.

  5. Click Next and enter the connection details. Refer to Token Authentication for more information on authentication configuration.

  6. Click Next. Select the data types that the collector will be collecting from Dynatrace

  7. Click Next. On the Account Data page, enter the following configurations.



    Request
    MethodGET
    URL/users
    Request Headers
    Acceptapplication/json
    AuthorizationBearer ${AuthenticationToken}
    Response Mappings
    AccountId$.items.*.email
    Id$.items.*.uid
    UserStatus$.items.*.userStatus
    LastLogin$.items.*.userLoginMetadata.lastSuccessfulLogin

  8. Click Next. On the Group Data page, enter the following configurations.



    Request
    MethodGET
    URL/groups
    Request Headers
    Acceptapplication/json
    AuthorizationBearer ${AuthenticationToken}
    Response Mappings
    GroupId$.items.*.name
    Id$.items.*.uuid
    Description$.items.*.description
  9. Click Next. On the Account Group Membership page, enter the following configurations.



    Request
    MethodGET
    URL/groups/${Id}/users
    Request Headers
    Acceptapplication/json
    AuthorizationBearer ${AuthenticationToken}
    Response Mappings
    Account Identifier$.data.*.id
  10. Click Next. In the Map Collector Attributes to Account Attributes, map the collected account attributes.

  11. Click Next. In the Map Collector Attributes to Account Mapping Attributes, map the user reference attribute.

  12. Click Next. In the Map Collector Attributes to Group Attributes, map the collected group attributes.

  13. Click Next. In the User Resolution Rules, map the user to account.

  14. Click Next. In the Member Account Resolution Rules, map the account.

💡 Info: This value MUST always be mapped to the Account Name for any generic REST collector.

  1. Click Finish. Use the Test function to make sure the configurations are accurate.

Application Setup​

This section explains the process of setting up a service account in Dynatrace for use within the collections process.

Steps

  1. Login to Dynatrace Account Management console https://myaccount.dynatrace.com/account/home.
  2. Under Identity & access management, click on OAuth clients.

  3. Click on Create client.

  4. Provide a valid (existing / Active) user email address and a meaningful description. We will restrict permissions to view user and groups data.

  5. Click Create client.

  6. Copy the client ID, client secret and account ID (portion after urn:daccount:).

Token Authentication​

This section explains the process of setting up RSA G&L ADC with token authentication.

Steps

  1. Login to RSA G&L console as System Administrator.
  2. Create or Edit a generic REST ADC that will be using Token authentication.
  3. On the Connection Details page, configure the following.



    Request
    MethodPOST
    Token URLhttps://sso.dynatrace.com/sso/oauth2/token
    UsernameClient ID
    PasswordClient Secret
    Request Headers
    Acceptapplication/json
    Content-Typeapplication/x-www-form-urlencoded
    Request Body
    grant_type=client_credentials&client_id=${TokenUsername}&client_secret=${TokenPassword}&scope=account-idm-read
    Response Mappings
    Response Path$.access_token
  4. Save the collector configuration & test.
  1. Dynatrace API

    ↩

entry.title: Collections - PingOne
entry.summary: PingOne is an identity and access management (IAM) solution by Ping Identity. It offers secure user authentication, authorization, and single sign-on across applications. With advanced features like multifactor authentication and adaptive security, PingOne ensures streamlined access control while enhancing user experience and safeguarding sensitive data.Identities ❌ | Accounts ✔️ | Groups ✔️ | App Roles ❌ | Entitlements ❌
entry.content:
PingOne is an identity and access management (IAM) solution by Ping Identity. It offers secure user authentication, authorization, and single sign-on across applications. With advanced features like multifactor authentication and adaptive security, PingOne ensures streamlined access control while enhancing user experience and safeguarding sensitive data.

Identities ❌ | Accounts ✔️ | Groups ✔️ | App Roles ❌ | Entitlements ❌

Table of Contents​

  • Application
  • Account Collector
  • Application Setup
  • Token Authentication

Application​

Steps

  1. Login to RSA G&L console as System Administrator.
  2. Navigate to Resources > Application.
  3. Click on Create Application and select Other Application.
  4. Enter the details as shown below.
  5. Click Finish.

Account Collector​

This section explains the process for configuring an account collector for the PingOne application. We will use the RSA G&L Generic REST collector API for this purpose.

Steps

  1. Login to RSA G&L console as System Administrator.
  2. Navigate to Resources > Applications.
  3. Click on PingOne.
  4. Navigate to Collectors tab and click on Create Account Collector. Enter the details as shown below.

  5. Click Next and enter the connection details. PingOne supports both OAuth and API key based authentication. We will token authentication for our collectors in this article. Refer to Token Authentication section.

  6. Click Next. Select the data types that the collector will be collecting from PingOne

  7. Click Next. On the Account Data page, enter the following configurations.



    Request
    MethodGET
    URL/users
    Request Headers
    Acceptapplication/json
    AuthorizationBearer ${AuthenticationToken}
    Response Mappings
    AccountId$._embedded.users.*.email
    Id$._embedded.users.*.id
    Status$._embedded.users.*.verifyStatus
    IsEnabled$._embedded.users.*.enabled
    UserName$._embedded.users.*.username

  8. Click Next. On the Group Data page, enter the following configurations.



    Request
    MethodGET
    URL/groups
    Request Headers
    Acceptapplication/json
    AuthorizationBearer ${AuthenticationToken}
    Response Mappings
    GroupId$._embedded.groups.*.name
    Id$._embedded.groups.*.id
    Description$._embedded.groups.*.description
    Owner$._embedded.groups.*.customData.groupOwner
  9. Click Next. On the Account Group Membership page, enter the following configurations.



    Request
    MethodGET
    URL/users/${Id}
    Query Parameters
    includememberOfGroupIDs
    Request Headers
    Acceptapplication/json
    AuthorizationBearer ${AuthenticationToken}
    Response Mappings
    Group Identifier$.memberOfGroupIDs.*
  10. Click Next. On the Subgroup Membership page, enter the following configurations.



    Request
    MethodGET
    URL/groups/${Id}/memberOfGroups
    Request Headers
    Acceptapplication/json
    AuthorizationBearer ${AuthenticationToken}
    Response Mappings
    Subgroup Identifier$._embedded.groupMemberships.*.id
  11. Click Next. In the Map Collector Attributes to Account Attributes, map the collected account attributes.

  12. Click Next. In the Map Collector Attributes to Account Mapping Attributes, map the user reference attribute.

  13. Click Next. In the Map Collector Attributes to Group Attributes, map the collected group attributes.

  14. Click Next. In the User Resolution Rules, map the user to account.

  15. Click Next. In the Member Account Resolution Rules, map the account attribute.

  16. Click Next. In the Sub-group Resolution Rules, map the group attribute.

  17. Click Next. In the Group Owner Resolution Rules, map the group attribute.

  18. Click Finish. Use the Test function to make sure the configurations are accurate.

Application Setup​

This section explains the process of setting up a service account in PingOne for use within the collections process.

Steps

  1. Login to PingOne console https://console.pingone.com/index.html
  2. Navigate to Connections > Applications.

  3. Click on Add Applications.

  4. Enter a suitable name and description and select application type as Worker.

  5. Click on Save.
  6. Click on the toggle to enable the application. Click on Grant Roles to add the necessary roles for the integration account.

  7. Add Identity Data Read Only Permissions roles and click Save.

  8. Click on Configuration tab and copy the Client ID and Client Secret.

Token Authentication​

This section explains the process of setting up RSA G&L ADC with token authentication.

Steps

  1. Login to RSA G&L console as System Administrator.
  2. Create or Edit a generic REST ADC that will be using Token authentication.
  3. On the Connection Details page, configure the following.



    Request
    MethodPOST
    Token URLhttps://auth.pingone.com/YOUR_ENV_ID/as/token
    UsernameNA
    PasswordNA
    Request Headers
    Acceptapplication/json
    Content-Typeapplication/x-www-form-urlencoded
    AuthorizationBasic AUTH_STRING

    AUTH_STRING - The Base64 encoded string of Client_ID:Client_Secret. The REST API to get the token requires basic authentication to be setup, instead of standard practice of sending the credentials in the POST body.
    Request Body
    grant_type=client_credentials
    Response Mappings
    Response Path$.access_token
  4. Save the collector configuration & test.
  1. PingOne API

    ↩

entry.title: Collections - Qlik
entry.summary: A Qlik application is a powerful data analytics tool that enables users to visualize and explore data for insightful decision-making. It facilitates interactive data discovery, real-time analysis, and dynamic dashboards. Qlik applications offer a user-friendly interface to create, manipulate, and share data-driven insights, fostering better understanding of trends and patterns within data.Identities ❌ | Accounts ✔️ | Groups ✔️ | App Roles ✔️ | Entitlements ❌
entry.content:
A Qlik application is a powerful data analytics tool that enables users to visualize and explore data for insightful decision-making. It facilitates interactive data discovery, real-time analysis, and dynamic dashboards. Qlik applications offer a user-friendly interface to create, manipulate, and share data-driven insights, fostering better understanding of trends and patterns within data.

Identities ❌ | Accounts ✔️ | Groups ✔️ | App Roles ✔️ | Entitlements ❌

Users are individuals who interact with Qlik applications to analyze, visualize, and gain insights from data. Each user has a unique account and login credentials to access the application. Users can have different roles and permissions based on their responsibilities within the organization.

Groups are collections of users who share similar roles or responsibilities. They enable efficient management of permissions and access control. Users within the same group often require the same level of access to data and functionalities. Grouping users simplifies administration and ensures consistency in permissions.

Roles define specific sets of permissions that determine what actions users can perform within the application. Roles can include privileges like creating, editing, or deleting data, as well as administrative capabilities. By assigning roles to users or groups, organizations ensure that users have the appropriate level of access and functionality based on their roles or responsibilities.

In Qlik applications, users are typically organized into groups. Roles are then assigned to these groups to define the permissions and actions that users within the group can undertake. This hierarchical approach streamlines access management: by modifying roles assigned to a group, permissions are automatically adjusted for all users within that group. This ensures data security, simplifies access control, and maintains consistency in permissions across the organization.

Table of Contents​

  • Application
  • Account Collector
  • Entitlement Collector
  • Application Setup

Application​

Steps

  1. Login to RSA G&L console as System Administrator.
  2. Navigate to Resources > Application.
  3. Click on Create Application and select Other Application.
  4. Enter the details as shown below.
  5. Click Finish.

Account Collector​

This section explains the process for configuring an account collector for the Qlik application. We will use the RSA G&L Generic REST collector API for this purpose.

Steps

  1. Login to RSA G&L console as System Administrator.
  2. Navigate to Resources > Applications.
  3. Click on Qlik.
  4. Navigate to Collectors tab and click on Create Account Collector. Enter the details as shown below.

  5. Click Next and enter the connection details.

  6. Click Next. Select the data types that the collector will be collecting from Qlik

  7. Click Next. On the Account Data page, enter the following configurations.




    Request
    MethodGET
    URL/api/v1/users
    Query Parameters
    fieldsid,email,status
    Request Headers
    Acceptapplication/json
    AuthorizationBearer ${AuthenticationToken}
    Response Mappings
    AccountId$.data.*.email
    Id$.data.*.id
    Status$.data.*.status

  8. Click Next. On the Group Data page, enter the following configurations.




    Request
    MethodGET
    URL/api/v1/groups
    Request Headers
    Acceptapplication/json
    AuthorizationBearer ${AuthenticationToken}
    Response Mappings
    GroupId$.data.*.name
    Id$.data.*.id
    Status$.data.*.status
  9. Click Next. On the Account Group Membership page, enter the following configurations.




    Request
    MethodGET
    URL/api/v1/users
    Query Parameters
    fieldsid,assignedGroups
    Request Headers
    Acceptapplication/json
    AuthorizationBearer ${AuthenticationToken}
    Response Mappings
    Account Identifier$.data.*.id
    Group IdentifierGroup Identifier $.data.*.assignedGroups.*.id
  10. Click Next. In the Map Collector Attributes to Account Attributes, map the collected account attributes.

  11. Click Next. In the Map Collector Attributes to Account Mapping Attributes, map the user reference attribute.

  12. Click Next. In the Map Collector Attributes to Group Attributes, map the collected group attributes.

  13. Click Next. In the User Resolution Rules, map the user to account.

  14. Click Next. In the Member Account Resolution Rules, map the account.

💡 Info: This value MUST always be mapped to the Account Name for any generic REST collector.

  1. Click Finish. Use the Test function to make sure the configurations are accurate.

Entitlement Collector​

This section explains the process for configuring an entitlement collector to collect roles from Qlik application. We will use the RSA G&L Generic REST collector for this purpose.

Steps

  1. Login to RSA G&L console as AveksaAdmin.
  2. Navigate to Resources > Applications.
  3. Click on Qlik.
  4. Navigate to Collectors tab and click on Create Entitlement Collector. Enter the details as shown below.

  5. Click Next and enter the connection details as shown in Step 5 of Account Collector.
  6. Click Next. Select the data types that the collector will be collecting from Qlik.

  7. Click Next. On the App Role Data page, enter the following configurations.




    Request
    MethodGET
    URL/api/v1/roles
    Request Headers
    Acceptapplication/json
    AuthorizationBearer ${AuthenticationToken}
    Response Mappings
    ApplicationRoleId$.data.*.name
    Id$.data.*.id
    Description$.data.*.description
  8. Click Next. On the App-Role Account Membership collection page, enter the following configurations.




    Request
    MethodGET
    URL/api/v1/users
    Query Parameters
    fieldsid,assignedRoles
    Request Headers
    Acceptapplication/json
    AuthorizationBearer ${AuthenticationToken}
    Response Mappings
    Application Role Id$.data.*.assignedRoles.*.id
    Account Identifier$.data.*.id
  9. Click Next. On the App-Role Group Membership Data page, enter the following configurations.




    Request
    MethodGET
    URL/api/v1/groups
    Request Headers
    Acceptapplication/json
    AuthorizationBearer ${AuthenticationToken}
    Response Mappings
    Application Role Id$.data.*.assignedRoles.*.id
    Account Identifier$.data.*.id
  10. Click Next. On the App-Role Account Membership collection page, enter the following configurations.




  11. Click Next. In the Map Collector Attributes to App Role Attributes, map the collected account attributes.
  12. Click Next. In the Account Evaluation, map the account collector to account value.

  13. Click Next and then click Finish. Use the Test function to make sure the configurations are accurate

Application Setup​

This section explains the process of setting up a service account in Qlik for use within the collections process.

Steps

  1. Login to Qlik Management console https://instance.qlikcloud.com/console/home.
  2. By default, API keys are disabled. Navigate to Settings and, under API Keys, select Enable API Keys.

  3. Navigate to API Keys and click on Generate New.
  4. Click on Generate New Key.
  5. Provide a suitable name and an expiration period for the key.

  6. Click Generate
  7. Copy and retain the API key.
  1. Qlik APIs

    ↩

entry.title: Collections - Freshservice
entry.summary: Freshservice is a cloud-based IT service management platform that streamlines and automates IT tasks. It offers a user-friendly interface, ticketing system, asset management, and reporting tools. With its intuitive design, Freshservice enables organizations to efficiently manage their IT services, track incidents, and maintain a well-organized IT infrastructure.Identities ❌ | Accounts ✔️ | Groups ✔️ | App Roles ✔️ | Entitlements ❌
entry.content:
Freshservice is a cloud-based IT service management platform that streamlines and automates IT tasks. It offers a user-friendly interface, ticketing system, asset management, and reporting tools. With its intuitive design, Freshservice enables organizations to efficiently manage their IT services, track incidents, and maintain a well-organized IT infrastructure.

Identities ❌ | Accounts ✔️ | Groups ✔️ | App Roles ✔️ | Entitlements ❌

Users are individuals who interact with the Freshservice platform. They can submit tickets, access knowledge articles, and perform various actions based on their assigned roles and permissions. Each user has a unique account tied to their email, and they can be assigned to specific groups or roles.

Groups are collections of users with similar roles, responsibilities, or access requirements. By grouping users together, you can streamline permissions and ensure that specific information or functionalities are available to the right people. For instance, you might have a "Support Team" group that has access to support-related features.

Roles define the level of access and permissions a user or a group of users has within Freshservice. Roles determine what actions users can perform and what information they can view or modify. Freshservice typically offers predefined roles like agents, admins, and requesters, each with distinct sets of privileges.

Table of Contents​

  • Application
  • Account Collector
  • Entitlement Collector
  • Application Setup

Application​

Steps

  1. Login to RSA G&L console as System Administrator.
  2. Navigate to Resources > Application.
  3. Click on Create Application and select Other Application.
  4. Enter the details as shown below.
  5. Click Finish.

Account Collector​

This section explains the process for configuring an account collector for the Freshservice application. We will use the RSA G&L Generic REST collector API for this purpose.

Steps

  1. Login to RSA G&L console as System Administrator.
  2. Navigate to Resources > Applications.
  3. Click on Freshservice.
  4. Navigate to Collectors tab and click on Create Account Collector. Enter the details as shown below.

  5. Click Next and enter the connection details. Freshservice supports Basic Access Authorization only with API key Authentication. The API key will be configured as user name and you can provide arbitrary password.

  6. Click Next. Select the data types that the collector will be collecting from Freshservice

  7. Click Next. On the Account Data page, enter the following configurations.




    Request
    MethodGET
    URL/api/v2/agents
    Request Headers
    Acceptapplication/json
    Response Mappings
    AccountId$.agents[*].email
    Id$.agents[*].id
    ExternalID$.agents[*].external_id

  8. Click Next. On the Group Data page, enter the following configurations.




    Request
    MethodGET
    URL/api/v2/groups
    Request Headers
    Acceptapplication/json
    Response Mappings
    GroupId$.groups[*].name
    Id$.groups[*].id
    Description$.groups[*].description
  9. Click Next. On the Account Group Membership page, enter the following configurations.



    Request
    MethodGET
    URL/api/v2/groups/${Id}
    Request Headers
    Acceptapplication/json
    Response Mappings
    AccountId$.group.members.*
  10. Click Next. In the Map Collector Attributes to Account Attributes, map the collected account attributes.

  11. Click Next. In the Map Collector Attributes to Account Mapping Attributes, map the user reference attribute.

  12. Click Next. In the Map Collector Attributes to Group Attributes, map the collected group attributes.

  13. Click Next. In the User Resolution Rules, map the user to account.

  14. Click Next. In the Member Account Resolution Rules, map the account.

💡 Info: This value MUST always be mapped to the Account Name for any generic REST collector.

  1. Click Finish. Use the Test function to make sure the configurations are accurate.

Entitlement Collector​

This section explains the process for configuring an entitlement collector to collect roles from Freshservice application. We will use the RSA G&L Generic REST collector for this purpose.

Steps

  1. Login to RSA G&L console as AveksaAdmin.
  2. Navigate to Resources > Applications.
  3. Click on Freshservice.
  4. Navigate to Collectors tab and click on Create Entitlement Collector. Enter the details as shown below.

  5. Click Next and enter the connection details as shown in Step 5 of Account Collector.
  6. Click Next. Select the data types that the collector will be collecting from Freshservice.

  7. Click Next. On the App Role Data page, enter the following configurations.




    Request
    MethodGET
    URL/api/v2/roles
    Request Headers
    Acceptapplication/json
    Response Mappings
    ApplicationRoleId$.roles[*].name
    Id$.roles[*].id
    Description$.roles[*].description
  8. Click Next. On the App-Role Account Membership collection page, enter the following configurations.




  9. Click Next. In the Map Collector Attributes to App Role Attributes, map the collected account attributes.
  10. Click Next. In the Account Evaluation, map the account collector to account value.

  11. Click Next and then click Finish. Use the Test function to make sure the configurations are accurate

Application Setup​

This section explains the process of setting up a service account in Freshservice for use within the collections process.

Steps

  1. Login to Freshservice dashboard.
  2. On the right hand corner, click on Profile Settings.

  3. Solve the captcha and copy the API key.

  1. API Reference

    ↩

  2. Freshservice authentication

    ↩
